Oktoberfest.de [![Image 1: Oktoberfest Munich (Go to homepage)](https://www.oktoberfest.de/themes/custom/muenchen/logo-oktoberfest-horizontal.svg)](https://www.oktoberfest.de/en) ![Image 2: Logo Landeshauptstadt M\u00fcnchen](https://www.oktoberfest.de/themes/custom/muenchen/logo-lhm-light.svg) Open main navigation *   Navigati", "relevance_score": 0.8}, {"title": "Five Fun Facts About Oktoberfest, The World\u2019s Largest Beer Festival", "url": "https://www.forbes.com/sites/dontse/2023/09/12/5-fun-facts-about-oktoberfest-the-worlds-largest-beer-festival/", "snippet": "[DDG Snippet] November 6, 2024 -Oktoberfest always starts on a Saturday in mid-September and runs to the first Sunday in October, a period of 16 days. But if that first Sunday falls on October 1 or October 2, the festival is extended until October 3, which is German Unity Day.", "relevance_score": 0.6}, {"title": "Oktoberfest facts and tips; Oktoberfest FAQ", "url": "https://oktoberfestpackage.com/facts-tips/", "snippet": "[DDG Snippet] December 16, 2024 -Week days are easier to get a seat in a tent but week nights are busy (and note that the last Thursday night of the fest is chock-a-block full). The larger the group you are, the more difficult it will be to find seats. Small groups of 2-3 persons are manageable (depending on when you go); larger groups should be prepared to split up or join one of our Oktoberfest packages.", "relevance_score": 0.6}]
